title: Yuko Miyamura at SMASH
wikitext:
[]: Yuko Miyamura at SMASH             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sun Aug 08, 2010 12:53 am So at SMASH! (Sydney Manga and Anime Show) this weekend I got the chance to ask a few rebuild related questions to Yuko Miyamura, the voice of Asuka. Unfortunately, she wasn't as forthcoming as I would have hoped. On the subject of when the next film will be released, she said that unfortunately the cast doesn't get told that sort of information, so she had no idea. When she was asked whether or not the characters Asuka Sohryu and Asuka Shikinami were the same character or not, she replied by saying that she asked Anno the very same question, only to be told that it was TOP SECRET. Finally, when I asked her what the significance of the 3 girls having the same ending of their names, "-nami", she said that she DID know why they all that the same name ending, but that she wasn't allowed to tell anyone because that was also TOP SECRET. So, maybe this will stir up some discussion. Here are the recordings (in TEEEEERRIBLE quality): Miyamura Panel : Where she talks about the character of Asuka (Shikinami and Sohryu) and her other work, and does some live voice acting. Evangelion Panel : Where everyone talks about acting performances and other random stuff. Grant & Greenfield Panel : Where Tiffany talks about what she thinks of the new Asuka, and also about her other roles and Matt talks about script writing and production stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Sun Aug 08, 2010 12:56 am Oh boy.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 08, 2010 1:10 am Thanks for posting this. You should totally make a news post about this btw, it's good stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sun Aug 08, 2010 1:25 am Oh man. I smell a bunch of retarded plot twists. Or Anno's just bullshitting her.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 08, 2010 1:29 am To be honest, I have the feeling Anno's bullshitting her too (I'm hoping that he's not and whatever he has up his sleeve will be interesting, but that's me being optimistic instead of ambivalent. :P), but I guess this is one of those "only time will tell" things. >_>

[]: Re: Yuko Miyamura at SMASH             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sun Aug 08, 2010 4:04 am Fazmotron wrote: Finally, when I asked her what the significance of the 3 girls having the same ending of their names, "-nami", she said that she DID know why they all that the same name ending, but that she wasn't allowed to tell anyone because that was also TOP SECRET.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 08, 2010 8:44 am Yuko-chan is so damn cute!! (And I agree with the fan and Yuko, Ogata-san is very エロかっこいい. ) Yuko-chan tries Engrish  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sun Aug 08, 2010 10:15 am Also, when asked what she thought Gendo said to Ritsuko in EoE, she said that maybe it was "you've gotten fat". P.S: Transcribing = UGH (but when I get the time....)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Aug 08, 2010 10:54 am If I only could speak Japanese T~T

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Sun Aug 08, 2010 12:03 pm In before 'lolhappyending'. Knowing the eva franchise, a happy ending (or an ending that makes sense, for that matter) would be blasphemy. However, it would be silly to imagine that the likelihood that Anno is telling the truth is anything but 50%.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Aug 08, 2010 12:22 pm Fazmotron wrote: Also, when asked what she thought Gendo said to Ritsuko in EoE, she said that maybe it was "you've gotten fat".

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Aug 08, 2010 12:46 pm SaltyJoe wrote: I have to wonder what Anno said to her about the *-nami deal. If it's more than "LOL they rhyme" then it might mean that Anno actually has a plan this time around.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Aug 08, 2010 1:56 pm esselfortium wrote: Yeah, I can't wait for the "Is Asuka Soryu a replicant, too?" threads to spill over into the general discussion forum in about a year.

[]: Re: Yuko Miyamura at SMASH             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Aug 08, 2010 2:10 pm Fazmotron wrote: TOP SECRET

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Aug 08, 2010 2:13 pm Maybe he just told Miyamura-san to say that so that he could watch us poor guys making silly theory. If anything, the rhyming names will have something to do with the prophecy thing

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 08, 2010 2:39 pm Sachi : I'm inclined to believe knowing what little we do know about NGE's production schedule and such, it's a mix of both where Anno/Kara might have general ideas of where to go (and/or keep any major plot-points to himself or a select few), but he still hasn't written that far ahead. Though for what it's worth, one of those 2007/2008 Newtype USA articles said that there was a general meeting before/during 1.0's production where all the films were outlined...but considering what we know that the schedule was getting pushed back, details properly were changed here and there. Feb 2007 Newtype USA wrote: The storyboards for the first movie have already been finished and sent off to the animators, so show director Hideaki Anno is already plotting out the scenario for the second film. Other names from the first series, including Masayuki, and Kazuya Tsurumaki, are also busily working away.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sun Aug 08, 2010 3:09 pm Azathoth wrote: Regardless of how much more passable her English is than Yamadera's, I'll never forgive the atrocities she was forced to perpetrate against German.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Aug 09, 2010 9:54 am March 2007 Newtype USA wrote: "Frankly, it just got too chaotic," Tsurumaki comments on the brainstorming sessions that were initially meant to provide an overall plot outline and final resolution to the story.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Mon Aug 09, 2010 10:49 am Amazing, despite Grave's Disease which nearly took her career down, it's still nice to hear that Yuko's still fighting. Thumbs-up for her.

[]:             Quote Post by darc_angel » Fri Aug 13, 2010 2:33 am Yuko Miyamura was an amazing guest! I was so glad I got to go to SMASH! My very first anime con and got to see really kool guests! Anyway, the live dubbing by her was truely a highlight.
